normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem preconditions {n : Nat} (w v : Fin n → Nat) (cap : Nat) : True
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume we are given equal-length weight and value arrays (modeled as functions on the same index type Fin n) and a non-negative capacity cap : Nat. (Trivial placeholder to anchor the formalization.)

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: def feasible (w v : Fin n → Nat) (cap : Nat) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : Prop := (∀ i ∈ S, True) ∧ (∑ i in S, w i) ≤ cap
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define feasibility: a chosen set of indices S is feasible iff its total weight is ≤ capacity (and S is a subset of valid indices).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: def totalValue (v : Fin n → Nat) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : Nat := ∑ i in S, v i
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the total value of a chosen set S as the sum of values over indices in S.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: def optValue (w v : Fin n → Nat) (cap : Nat) : Nat := sSup { totalValue v S | S : Finset (Fin n) // feasible w v cap S }
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the optimal value as the supremum (maximum) of totalValue over all feasible subsets. (In Nat, this corresponds to the maximum achievable value.)

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem optValue_is_max {w v : Fin n → Nat} {cap : Nat} : ∃ S : Finset (Fin n), feasible w v cap S ∧ totalValue v S = optValue w v cap
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Show that the supremum is attained: there exists a feasible subset achieving the optimal value.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
-- Let `knap` be the Lean function extracted/defined to match the Rust reference implementation.
-- Obligation: `knap w v cap` computes some DP-based result.
variable (knap : (Fin n → Nat) → (Fin n → Nat) → Nat → Nat)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Introduce a Lean function `knap` corresponding to the Rust reference implementation (DP knapsack).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem knap_correctness_shape (w v : Fin n → Nat) (cap : Nat) : True
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Anchor the DP structure: the implementation returns a Nat computed from weights/values and capacity via a recurrence over items and capacities.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
-- For each prefix of items and each capacity bound, the DP table entry equals the optimum over feasible subsets of that prefix.
-- Let `dp` be the DP table used by `knap`.
variable (dp : Nat → Nat → Nat) -- dp i c = best value using first i items with capacity c

theorem dp_invariant (w v : Fin n → Nat) (cap : Nat) :
  ∀ i c : Nat, c ≤ cap →
    dp i c = optValue (w ∘ Fin.subtype (fun j => j < i)) (v ∘ Fin.subtype (fun j => j < i)) c
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: State the core DP invariant: for each prefix length i and capacity c, the DP entry equals the optimal value achievable using only the first i items.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem dp_base (w v : Fin n → Nat) (cap : Nat) :
  ∀ c : Nat, c ≤ cap → dp 0 c = 0
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Base case: with zero items, the best achievable value is 0 for any capacity.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem dp_step (w v : Fin n → Nat) (cap : Nat) :
  ∀ i c : Nat, i < n → c ≤ cap →
    let wi := w ⟨i, by exact Nat.lt_of_lt_of_le (Nat.lt_succ_self i) (Nat.lt_of_lt_of_le (Nat.lt_succ_self i) (Nat.le_refl _))⟩ in
    let vi := v ⟨i, by exact Nat.lt_succ_self i⟩ in
    dp (i+1) c =
      if wi ≤ c then max (dp i c) (dp i (c - wi) + vi) else dp i c
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Inductive recurrence: when considering item i, either skip it (value dp i c) or take it if it fits (value dp i (c - wi) + vi), and take the maximum.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem feasible_prefix_subset (w v : Fin n → Nat) (cap : Nat) (S : Finset (Fin n)) (i : Nat) :
  feasible w v cap S →
  feasible (w ∘ fun j => (⟨j.1, j.2⟩ : Fin i)) (v ∘ fun j => (⟨j.1, j.2⟩ : Fin i)) cap (S.filter (fun k => k.1 < i))
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Show that restricting a feasible set to a prefix of items yields a feasible set for that prefix.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem dp_is_upper_bound (w v : Fin n → Nat) (cap : Nat) :
  ∀ S : Finset (Fin n), feasible w v cap S →
    totalValue v S ≤ knap w v cap
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Soundness (upper bound): the implementation’s result is at least as large as the value of any feasible subset.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem dp_is_lower_bound (w v : Fin n → Nat) (cap : Nat) :
  ∃ S : Finset (Fin n), feasible w v cap S ∧ totalValue v S = knap w v cap
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Completeness (lower bound): there exists a feasible subset whose value equals the implementation’s result.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem knap_correct (w v : Fin n → Nat) (cap : Nat) :
  knap w v cap = optValue w v cap
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Final specification: the Rust implementation returns exactly the optimal knapsack value.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem knap_edge_cases (w v : Fin n → Nat) :
  knap w v 0 = 0 ∧ knap w v (0) = 0
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Edge case: with capacity 0, the only feasible subset is the empty set, so the result is 0.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem each_item_used_at_most_once (w v : Fin n → Nat) (cap : Nat) :
  ∀ S : Finset (Fin n), feasible w v cap S →
    (∀ i : Fin n, i ∈ S → True)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Model that each item can be selected at most once by representing selections as a Finset (no duplicates). (Trivial in Finset model; ensures the subset interpretation.)
